page: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 architecture, utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 4.0 GB of memory, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is delivered via the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, with gzip compression reaching 30.29 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show clear limitations. The instance scores in the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth write operations and small-block cached reads also fall into the lower performance tiers, while Passmark CPU encryption and extended instructions tests register in the bottom 10%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c8gn.large offers a low-cost entry point into the Graviton8 family, balancing dedicated ARMv9 cores against a constrained 4.0 GB memory footprint. This resource density makes it suitable for single-threaded utilities, lightweight text processing, and basic database operations, whereas multi-threaded compilation, heavy image processing, and LLM inference are constrained by the low core count and memory capacity.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is a compute-optimized, ARM64-based instance featuring 2 dedicated physical cores powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors running at 2.8 GHz. Equipped with 4.0 GB of memory and a 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, this instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, particularly in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth write operations are relatively weak, as evidenced by lower tier stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core scores. The instance provides a cost-efficient entry point for workloads that prioritize single-core efficiency over high memory capacity, making it suitable for lightweight database operations, text processing, and single-threaded utility tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is a compute-optimized ARM64 instance with 2 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c8gn.large is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 2 dedicated Graviton cores, 4.0 GB RAM, and 6 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton processors, balanced for single-threaded tasks and lightweight network-optimized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 ARMv9 processor with 2 dedicated cores
Equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory and 6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Delivers strong single-threaded performance in compression and decompression benchmarks
Lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage
Suited for lightweight database operations, text processing, and single-threaded tasks
